summary.en: The creative and bohemian soul of Jacksonville, where grand historic homes and riverfront parks meet a vibrant, gritty hub of indie theaters, funky boutiques, and sought-after eateries.
Riverside’s polished, sophisticated older sibling, known for its immaculate historic homes, serene tree-lined streets, and a chic, walkable shopping district.
A charming, European-inspired neighborhood centered around a picturesque square with iconic lion sculptures, art galleries, and upscale restaurants.
Jacksonville's oldest neighborhood, a historic district defined by stunning late 19th-century homes and a proud, community-driven revitalization.
An unpretentious, quirky, and cool historic neighborhood that has become a haven for artists and culinary entrepreneurs, known for its vibrant murals and retro feel.
The urban heart of the city, a place of contrasts combining the Northbank's business district and architectural gems with the Southbank's scenic parks and skyline views.

description.en: A bustling corner cafe perfect for people-watching, known for strong coffee, egg sandwiches, and viral cake slices.
An intimate, seven-table restaurant offering a unique dining experience with Spanish and Mediterranean tapas. Reservations are essential.
A breathtaking 5.8-acre park on the St. Johns River, designed by the Olmsted Brothers and anchored by the iconic 'Life' sculpture.
A sprawling market of local artists and food vendors held every Saturday under the Fuller Warren Bridge, feeling like the city's weekly block party.

name.en: Cummer Museum of Art and Gardens

description.en: While the museum is well-known, its two acres of historic gardens on the river are a perfect, tranquil escape from the city buzz.
The real magic of Avondale is in its side streets, where you can wander and admire stunning, multi-million dollar historic homes with unique architectural details.
Public access points east of the shopping district offer quiet, tree-framed views of the water that feel like a private discovery.
A beloved local ice cream shop known for its inventive, handcrafted flavors like 'Ice Box Lemon' and 'Coffee + Donuts'.
Walk north from the square to connect with the Southbank Riverwalk for some of the city's best skyline views, especially at sunset.
The quintessential neighborhood cafe of Springfield, this cozy spot serves delicious pastries, breads, and savory lunch items with a focus on local ingredients.
A sprawling green space featuring a unique Venetian-style promenade designed by famed Prairie School architect Henry J. Klutho.
Springfield is a treasure trove for architecture buffs, home to one of the largest collections of Prairie School buildings outside the Midwest.
The historic heart of Springfield's commerce is experiencing a rebirth, with historic buildings being adapted into new, independent businesses.
A vast park on the neighborhood's southern edge that offers wide-open spaces and a surprisingly good vantage point for photos of the downtown skyline.
An essential Murray Hill institution, this artisan bakery and cafe is known for its incredible sourdough bread, pastries, and hearty sandwiches.
Operated by the Art League of Jacksonville, this center is a hub of community creativity, offering classes and workshops.
The iconic marquee of this historic theater is a landmark of the neighborhood, representing its long history as a community gathering place.
A pioneering craft brewery in the sports district with a massive, popular taproom and a rooftop bar offering great views.
A downtown brewery with a welcoming taproom, known for its solid lineup of beers and a friendly, local vibe.
A fantastic to-go pie shop known for its creative sweet and savory pies, sold by the slice or as a 'pie flight'.
A beloved BBQ joint on the Southbank. Grab a platter of smoked meats and enjoy the casual atmosphere before a walk along the river.
A majestic 250-year-old Southern live oak that is older than the city itself, this tree is a true Jacksonville legend.
This massive, beautifully restored fountain offers spectacular light and water shows at night, with the downtown skyline as a backdrop.
A collection of three historic, early 20th-century skyscrapers that stand as a beautiful monument to Jacksonville's 'first skyscraper era'.
Miles of walking paths on both the Northbank and Southbank offer fantastic views of the city and river.
The walk across this iconic blue lift bridge offers unparalleled 360-degree views of the city and the St. Johns River.

prompt.en: Capture a photo that you think perfectly represents the creative and bohemian soul of this neighborhood.
Find a street with a beautiful canopy of oak trees and take a photo looking up through the branches.
Find an interesting architectural detail on one of the grand historic homes—a unique window, porch column, or doorway—and take a close-up shot.
Take a picture of the most interesting or funky boutique window display you can find.
Find a spot with a view of the St. Johns River and take a photo that includes the water.
Discover a piece of street art or a mural and take a photo that tells a story.
Take a photo of your coffee or snack from a local cafe, with the neighborhood vibe in the background.
Capture a single image that shows the neighborhood's mix of historic preservation and youthful, gritty energy.
Find a good spot for people-watching and take a photo that captures the flow of life in the neighborhood.
Search for and photograph an unusual or quirky architectural feature on a building.
Find a quiet park bench and take a photo from its perspective, capturing a moment of peace.
If you're here at the right time, capture the colors of the sunset over the river.
Take a selfie that shows you soaking in the creative, youthful energy of Five Points.
Many historic homes have beautiful gardens. Find one you can see from the street and capture a glimpse of its beauty.
Find a cafe that looks like the perfect place to spend an afternoon and snap a photo of its exterior.
Take a photo of a house or building that you feel best represents Avondale's polished and sophisticated vibe.
Find the most beautifully manicured lawn or garden and capture its perfect details.
Look for a home with Mediterranean Revival features (stucco, red tile roof) and photograph a specific detail like an arched window or doorway.
Take a selfie on one of Avondale's serene, tree-lined streets.
Capture a long shot down one of the neighborhood's quiet streets, emphasizing the trees and historic homes.
Photograph a window display from one of the high-end boutiques in the Shoppes of Avondale.
Take a photo that conveys a sense of quiet, elegant luxury.
Try to find a home with Prairie School architecture and capture its strong horizontal lines.
Find an example of decorative ironwork on a gate, fence, or window and get a close-up shot.
Photograph the most grand and stately front door or entrance you can find.
Use the reflection in a boutique window to capture the street scene behind you.
Venture off the main avenue and capture the peaceful atmosphere of a residential side street.
Find a building with perfect symmetry and take a photo that highlights its balanced design.
Find a spot where you can see the St. Johns River between the houses and trees, and capture that hidden view.
Take a photo of a local delicacy or drink that looks as polished and picturesque as the neighborhood itself.
Capture a photo that makes San Marco look like a small town square in Europe.
Take a creative photo of one of the iconic lion sculptures. Try a low angle to make it look majestic.
Get a shot of the central fountain, focusing on the water in motion.
Find the most appealing boutique and take a photo of its window display.
Take a photo of your drink or ice cream with the San Marco square in the background.
Photograph the exterior of one of the neighborhood's art galleries.
Walk towards the river and capture the best view of the downtown skyline you can find.
Explore the side streets off the main square and find a charming, picturesque alleyway to photograph.
Find a small, interesting detail within San Marco Square—a piece of tile, a park bench, a lamp post—and make it the subject of your photo.
Take a photo of the entrance to a theater or restaurant that looks dramatic and inviting.
Capture the sophisticated facade of one of San Marco's upscale restaurants.
Take a selfie where you look as sophisticated and friendly as the neighborhood feels.
If you're here in the evening, capture the warm glow of the lights around the square.
Many buildings here have beautiful arches. Find your favorite one and frame a photo with it.
Take a photo of a scene that captures the friendly, welcoming atmosphere of San Marco.
Find a restored Victorian home with multiple colors and capture a photo that highlights its intricate, 'painted lady' details.
Take a photo that shows the neighborhood's revitalization in action—perhaps an old building next to a new cafe, or a home mid-restoration.
The porches in Springfield are stunning. Find one that catches your eye and take a photo that emphasizes its size and character.
Find a building with Prairie School features (strong horizontal lines, overhanging eaves) and capture its unique design.
Take a selfie that captures the authentic, community-driven vibe of Springfield.
Find a detail—like an old sign, a historical marker, or a piece of architecture—that feels like a window into the neighborhood's long history.
Walk through one of the parks and take a photo along a path or promenade that shows off the green space.
Capture a single image showing a historic building being used for a modern purpose, like a new brewery or cafe.
Take a photo of the Main Street corridor that shows its ongoing commercial rebirth.
Find a vantage point in one of the parks and get a shot of the downtown skyline.
Take a photo of a scene that you feel captures the authentic, historic, and unpretentious character of Springfield.
Use the branches of one of Springfield's massive, old trees to frame a shot of a historic home.
Get a close-up photo of the detailed woodwork (gingerbread trim, spindles, etc.) on a Victorian-era house.
Photograph a house that looks like it has a hundred years of stories to tell.
Capture the cozy, welcoming exterior of a neighborhood bakery or cafe.
Find your favorite mural on Edgewood Avenue and take a creative photo of it.
Find a cool, retro-looking sign on a storefront and capture its vintage vibe.
Take a selfie that captures the quirky, unpretentious, and unapologetically cool feel of Murray Hill.
Photograph the most unique or quirky independent shop you can find.
Take a photo looking down Edgewood Avenue that captures the energy and color of the street.
Find a small, artistic detail on the street—not a huge mural, but something smaller—and make it your photo's subject.
Capture an image of a building or scene that hints at the neighborhood's historic, working-class feel.
Take a picture of the most exciting or delicious-looking food you find in the neighborhood.
Peer into a vintage or record store and take a photo of an item that catches your eye.
This neighborhood is full of color. Take a photo where one bright color is the dominant subject.
Find a place that looks like a hub for artists or makers and capture its creative exterior.
Use a colorful mural as a backdrop for a portrait of a friend or a creative selfie.
Take a photo of a landmark, like the Murray Hill Theatre marquee, that defines the neighborhood.
Photograph a scene of people enjoying themselves in a casual, relaxed way that sums up the local vibe.
Look down the alleys off Edgewood Avenue to find and photograph a hidden or less-obvious piece of street art.
Find a modern building and capture the reflection of another skyscraper or the sky in its glass.
Take a single photo that shows a historic architectural gem right next to a gleaming modern skyscraper.
Use your phone's panorama mode to capture a wide shot of the skyline from the Riverwalk.
Take a photo that focuses on the interesting architectural lines and structure of one of the downtown bridges.
Capture a photo that represents the energy and buzz of the city's urban heart.
From the Southbank, take your best shot of the Northbank skyline across the river.
If you're near Friendship Fountain at night, try to capture the colorful light and water show.
Take a selfie with the massive Treaty Oak, trying to show its incredible scale.
Photograph a scene that captures the professional, business-like atmosphere of the Northbank.
Find a piece of public art or a sculpture and take a creative photo of it.
Safely take a photo from one of the pedestrian walkways on a bridge, capturing the view of the city and river.
At night, capture the reflection of the city's lights on the surface of the St. Johns River.
Find one of the historic buildings, like those in the Laura Street Trio, and take a close-up of an ornate architectural detail.
Take a photo that shows the contrast between a peaceful park space and the tall city buildings surrounding it.
Take a photo from as close to the water's edge as you can get, looking up at the city skyline.
